114485.explanation: In the current system, voters appoint representatives who are expected to make a careful balancing of interests. For weighty social issues, a referendum could be among the options.

114489.explanation: What do we mean by sustainable energy? Ultimately, the residents will pay for it themselves.

114491.explanation: As long as the distribution law is in effect, every municipality must meet its quota. This applies to Goeree-Overflakkee as well. We meet our quota.

114493.explanation: Housing on one's own property is among the possibilities. If the agricultural entrepreneur does not have this option, use can be made of the large-scale reception location(s).

114501.explanation: GAAN realizes that this is difficult under current laws and regulations but supports the farmers in this development.

114503.explanation: The local broadcaster is part of a healthy democracy. GAAN finds this very important. The condition is that the local broadcaster reports on municipal politics.

114505.explanation: GAAN does not consider this a municipal responsibility.

114510.explanation: GAAN finds camera surveillance desirable at strategic locations where there is a lot of nuisance and safety is at risk.

114511.explanation: This is well organized through the central municipality of Nissewaard. Spaces for 2026 have even been expanded.

114515.explanation: GAAN believes this should be allowed in recreational parks where the recreational function has faded or is no longer present.

114523.explanation: GAAN is of the opinion that care provision is neutral and thus accessible to everyone, regardless of faith, culture, or beliefs.

114524.explanation: GAAN considers the price-quality ratio to be leading.

114525.explanation: GAAN finds this an overly easy lever to pull. The municipality should first get its basics in order.

114529.explanation: If there is no demand, stores do not need to be open. Organizing a shopping evening where all shops are open is already difficult. GAAN considers this the entrepreneur’s choice.

114532.explanation: GAAN supports solar panels on roofs.

114535.explanation: With a municipality surrounded by water, a swimming diploma is a requirement for every child.

114536.explanation: GAAN considers our dialect very important.

114538.explanation: What is high? It must fit within the environment. GAAN thinks of a maximum of five floors.

114539.explanation: Given the current housing problems, every home seeker who finds a place to live is a gain. Of course, this must comply with regulations.

114542.explanation: Where this already occurs, the municipality must look into it.

114543.explanation: GAAN believes this must be decided in consultation with the BIZ Ouddorp.

114546.explanation: GAAN believes housing must be built for all target groups.

114547.explanation: GAAN feels the municipality can create the preconditions to make this possible.

114485.explanation: The SGP is based on representative democracy (residents elect council members). The SGP is not a supporter of referendums.

114489.explanation: The SGP believes the municipal role in joint generation projects is facilitating and stimulating, but without providing financial support. This does apply to individual home sustainability efforts, for example through sustainability subsidies and loans.

114491.explanation: The municipality has a legal task in the reception of refugees. Additionally, refugee reception is a matter of Christian mercy. It must, however, be appropriate to the nature and scale of the community.

114493.explanation: The SGP is not against small-scale housing at companies, but that will not be sufficient. We have many labor migrants on our island, so a number of large-scale locations will be needed. The SGP prefers these in rural areas, properly integrated and with attention to their impact on town centers.

114501.explanation: Housing expansions take place on the edges of towns. Expansions take into account the landscape, greenery, and the area's cultural-historical layout. This demands plans that carefully balance growth with preserving the characteristic features of the environment.

114503.explanation: In the new broadcasting system, funding for regional broadcasters is arranged by the national government. The municipality has no resources to support local broadcasters.

114505.explanation: The municipality encourages cooperation between schools and music schools to make music education accessible to all children. Wonderful initiatives, such as school orchestras and joint playing projects, receive support where possible.

114509.explanation: Compliance with laws and regulations requires supervision. However, the police cannot be everywhere at once. Therefore, the municipality provides extra supervision and enforcement by deploying special enforcement officers (boa’s). They actively contribute, for example, to combating street nuisance.

114510.explanation: As far as the SGP is concerned, camera surveillance should only be used in very specific situations.

114511.explanation: Where possible, local problems are also addressed by local assistance (nearby and appropriate).

114515.explanation: Designation of recreational parks without recreational potential and where much permanent residence already occurs will be converted to housing, without this reducing the housing program. The SGP opposes mixing living and recreation in parks that have recreational potential.

114518.explanation: According to the SGP, exemption from municipal taxes belongs in a good minimum policy.

114521.explanation: Electric mobility continues to increase. The municipality facilitates and stimulates more locations for (fast) charging.

114522.explanation: Main roads generally remain 50 km/h unless traffic safety requires otherwise. In residential areas, the maximum speed is 30 km/h. These roads must also be designed according to guidelines for safe 30 km/h roads.

114523.explanation: The SGP is a strong supporter of freedom of choice in care, especially concerning beliefs.

114524.explanation: Of course, the costs of youth care are a cause for concern. At the same time, it is about providing the most appropriate care, preferably nearby.

114525.explanation: The SGP is cautious regarding tax increases. However, we will not avoid adjusting this lever if financial difficulties arise.

114529.explanation: God, in His wisdom, has given humans a day of rest. This day of rest is meant to honor God, serve Him, and look after one another. Sunday is the day of rest in the Christian tradition. Therefore, the SGP is fundamentally opposed to (sport) events and shops being open on this first day of the week.

114530.explanation: The national government sets the rules in this regard. Nonetheless, the SGP would like to consider temporary housing as an option for status holders to reduce pressure on regular housing.

114531.explanation: The current policy is to offer six free uses and then charge a small fee. As far as the SGP is concerned, this should remain unchanged.

114532.explanation: Solar energy on rooftops takes priority over solar fields. The municipality will not allocate space for solar panels on agricultural land, except when these are mobile, allowing for dual land use.

114535.explanation: Although it is important in our water-rich municipality that children learn to swim, the SGP does not see free swimming lessons as a municipal task. The municipality does facilitate swimming pools where swimming lessons are the top priority.

114536.explanation: Schools receive support for nature and cultural education. The municipality provides them with room for their own approach, fitting the school's identity.

114538.explanation: Because the Province enforces an overly rigid environmental policy, part of the housing development task must take place within the cores. Four to six residential layers can then be a solution, provided it fits the nature and scale of the core.

114539.explanation: The SGP would prefer more temporary homes to be added, either project-based and spread across the island or incidentally on people's own properties.

114542.explanation: Where there are bottlenecks, careful consideration must be given to how to solve them from a traffic perspective. Expansion is not necessarily needed everywhere.

114543.explanation: The existing agreements, made jointly with entrepreneurs, to ban cars at certain times and locations during specific periods, are acceptable to the SGP.

114546.explanation: The municipality, together with housing corporations and project developers, commits to an appropriate housing supply. The share of social rental, affordable homeownership, and mid-level rent is aligned with local demand.

114547.explanation: Although these functions are very important for the cores, the SGP believes the municipality is not the party to actually invest money in them. The municipality can act as a facilitator to help maintain or regain certain facilities.

114485.explanation: We support more citizen participation and early involvement of residents through village discussions, resident surveys, and neighborhood consultations on important decisions. But a referendum is not necessarily the best tool.

114489.explanation: The municipality facilitates permits, grid connections, and clear rules but does not distribute subsidies freely. We keep costs low and leave investments mainly to residents and market parties.

114491.explanation: We take responsibility for reception: humane, small-scale, flexible, and within capacity. We ensure clear and transparent communication to residents. Safety comes first with clear rules, enforcement, and swift handling of nuisance.

114493.explanation: Businesses are responsible for proper housing, and agricultural companies are given space for small-scale appropriate housing.

114501.explanation: Not permitted by default. In exceptions, customized solutions can be applied, for example in estate development or on the edge of a residential core. We remain careful with agricultural land and the landscape.

114503.explanation: We support a strong and independent local broadcaster but currently see no need for additional structural funding.

114505.explanation: Music is part of children's development and keeps associations strong. With a simple arrangement, every child can become acquainted, even if their home situation has limited means.

114509.explanation: A safe island requires visible community enforcement officers (boa’s), and we advocate for more neighborhood police officers and boa’s in all cores.

114510.explanation: Focus enforcement on places with nuisance and crime. With clear rules, evaluation, and respect for privacy, but action when necessary.

114511.explanation: Victims must always be able to quickly reach safety. This can be via a strong regional provision with good accessibility and clear local referral, not necessarily with a new building in our municipality.

114515.explanation: Each park will be examined to see if a temporary dual zoning or conversion to housing is possible, with requirements for building quality, management, safety, and livability. For residents, temporary occupancy of a vacation home with an environmental permit and conditions may be allowed bridging periods.

114518.explanation: We actively pursue policies for vulnerable groups and tackle poverty with quick and straightforward assistance, so remissions remain available.

114521.explanation: We focus on private charging where possible. We do not add extra charging parking spots everywhere, as that pressures scarce parking space. We place charging points targeted based on demand and usage data and keep spots flexible when possible to avoid them remaining empty.

114522.explanation: 30 km/h makes villages safer for children and cyclists. Main routes may keep exceptions where needed, but 30 km/h will become the norm.

114523.explanation: Everyone belongs, and care must align with people. Freedom of choice fits the human scale as long as quality is guaranteed.

114524.explanation: We steer healthcare procurement on quality and continuity, not primarily on the lowest price.

114525.explanation: We keep housing costs as low as possible and below the national average.

114529.explanation: Sunday remains a day for socializing, relaxation, family, and space for faith and reflection. Shops should not be forced, directly or indirectly, to open on Sundays to compete. Shop staff also deserve a guaranteed day off.

114530.explanation: To be fair: those who live here and have waited a long time should not be placed at the back of the queue. We stand for fair allocation and primarily build more affordable and flexible homes to reduce pressure for everyone.

114531.explanation: Free disposal of bulky household waste helps combat illegal dumping and keeps the island tidy. We maintain waste collection points in Goedereede and Middelharnis.

114532.explanation: Solar on rooftops is prioritized and not on agricultural land.

114535.explanation: The municipality must allocate funds for school swimming so every child learns to swim. This requires sufficient swimming water: we commit to a new swimming pool and to preserving the 't Zuiderdiep pool in Stellendam.

114536.explanation: Dialects and traditions belong to the identity of our villages and we want to keep them 'experiential'.

114538.explanation: Middelharnis must remain livable and retain its village character. High-rise flats do not fit here.

114539.explanation: Given the housing shortage, a total ban is too harsh and removes affordable housing. However, strict rules against abuse, overcrowding, and slumlords apply, with firm enforcement where it gets out of hand.

114542.explanation: We maintain vital centers with sufficient parking and ensure adequate parking spaces with new construction.

114543.explanation: Not car-free all year round. But car-reduced during the tourist season: from April 1 to October 31, no cars in the Ring between 11:00 and 23:00 for safety and space.

114546.explanation: Public housing means sufficient and affordable homes for starters, families, and elderly people in all villages.

114547.explanation: We strengthen facilities and meeting places close to home and build in all villages to ensure the future of these communities.

114485.explanation: Through village councils and participation processes, residents have the opportunity, besides elections, to make their voices heard. According to the VVD, village councils should have more influence. Council members are elected by residents to make the final decisions on their behalf.

114489.explanation: Local initiatives involving wind turbines or ground-mounted solar parks will not receive financial support from the municipality in our view. We will critically assess other collective initiatives. As the VVD, we choose a national solution with nuclear energy.

114491.explanation: We do not have large-scale asylum reception. We provide assistance to people fleeing war violence through boats and temporary flexible housing units.

114493.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have the possibility to house workers on their own premises so that homes in villages are no longer used for accommodating labor migrants.

114501.explanation: It is undesirable to build freely on agricultural land. We want to preserve the character of farmland. Uncontrolled construction of homes outside villages can also cause disruptions to the operations of nearby farms and businesses.

114503.explanation: In addition to existing subsidies, it is up to local media themselves to create a balanced budget by properly aligning their offerings and (advertising) revenues.

114505.explanation: Besides existing music projects at primary schools, the municipality does not provide extra subsidies for music lessons. For minor children, parents with limited financial resources have access to the Child Package, from which, for example, music lessons can be paid.

114509.explanation: There is a need for more supervision and enforcement to keep our island safe and livable for everyone.

114510.explanation: In places where a lot of nuisance is experienced, more (temporary) camera surveillance can be deployed.

114511.explanation: In cooperation with surrounding municipalities, this care is offered off the island to ensure accessibility and safety.

114515.explanation: In some holiday parks, where recreational use is none or limited, owners of holiday homes can live permanently. This helps us provide more people with a (temporary) home.

114518.explanation: Residents are responsible for paying bills, including municipal taxes. All users should contribute to the costs. Only in exceptional cases can the municipality temporarily contribute, for example through exemption from payment.

114521.explanation: Public charging stations should grow along with demand. However, charging spots take up parking spaces, so a good balance is necessary and not everywhere needs more chargers. Where possible, existing stations will be replaced by fast chargers for quicker charging and better flow.

114522.explanation: Where it is safe to drive 50 km/h within built-up areas, we should continue to do so.

114523.explanation: We do not discriminate based on religion, culture, or belief. The care demand is decisive. This must be the starting point from our municipality.

114524.explanation: Looking only at the price can result in failing to achieve the goals for which the care is engaged. We assess the best combination of supply, price, and quality.

114525.explanation: Raising local taxes is an easy way for the municipality to balance the budget. We want the municipality to manage its finances better before residents have to pay extra.

114529.explanation: Your Sunday, your choice! Residents and entrepreneurs are free to decide how they spend a Sunday, respecting the island’s culture. For us, this means entrepreneurs and organizers can open from 12:00 PM on Sundays.

114530.explanation: We do not want to make distinctions with our own residents.

114531.explanation: Residents continue to have the option of bringing waste to the recycling center for free a number of times per year.

114532.explanation: We choose nuclear energy and do not include solar power in this.

114535.explanation: The municipality has no primary task of paying for swimming lessons; swimming education is a parental responsibility. For minor children, parents with limited financial means can use the Child Package to pay for swimming lessons.

114536.explanation: Passing on the island’s culture, history, and language is important and should be addressed in every school. However, it is not the municipality’s task to facilitate this.

114538.explanation: Apartment complexes of up to 4 to 5 floors are a solution for the housing shortage and fit within our municipality. Taller residential towers do not fit in our rural area.

114539.explanation: Room rental can offer a solution for the tight housing market, within certain frameworks to keep it safe and without nuisance.

114542.explanation: Due to the location and accessibility, we have more cars per household than national standards indicate. New housing projects and redesigns of neighborhoods and center areas should consider higher standards to prevent major parking problems.

114543.explanation: For future-proof area development, the Ring of Ouddorp will be car-free, while taking into account the supply needs of businesses.

114546.explanation: We want to build for different target groups to better balance the housing market. Think of move-on homes for elderly, flexible housing for youth, and apartments. We build according to need, including affordable homes.

114547.explanation: The municipality cannot maintain everything. Facilities remain in villages if sufficiently used, so it remains profitable for entrepreneurs. Municipal financial support leads to large expenses and higher taxes for everyone.

114485.explanation: An advisory referendum can be useful in some cases, but many important subjects are complex and require in-depth examination. Then another form of participation/consultation seems wiser.

114489.explanation: It is positive if residents want to generate sustainable energy together, although we already generate a lot on our island. In our view, the municipality can facilitate/support these residents but financial support is not necessary.

114491.explanation: The two boats currently near our island are an emergency solution because the entire country is stuck regarding reception. As long as there is no improvement, we as a municipality must take our responsibility and offer these people a safe and hospitable place.

114493.explanation: Seasonal workers may, in our view, be housed as much as possible on their own premises, provided there are good agreements and controls to ensure decent housing.

114501.explanation: This is not preferred. Building on fertile agricultural land and/or natural areas should be avoided as much as possible. This with the caveat that it is not always avoidable given the housing shortage. Careful national planning should then be the starting point.

114503.explanation: It is important that the local broadcaster functions well and serves the community. Critical and adequate journalism is also important and of great value in our democracy. Funding will come from The Hague. The municipality can request and finance extra assignments.

114505.explanation: Almost all primary school pupils receive at least one year of music lessons at school; we want to maintain this. We also want to maintain contributions from the Child Package for music lessons. Every child should have the opportunity to get acquainted with and make music regardless of financial background.

114509.explanation: In our view, they are deployed to increase safety on the island and to combat nuisance (dog waste, parking nuisance, PMD waste, littering, event controls, vandalism, illegal habitation, etc.).

114510.explanation: Our beautiful island is fortunately still reasonably safe. Our residents look out for each other and watch over the neighborhood together. Camera surveillance also reduces privacy. We believe this should only be an option in places where nuisance occurs regularly.

114511.explanation: It is important that there are enough places where women and children can find a safe refuge if the home situation is unsafe. At the same time, women who prefer a shelter further away from the island for reasons of anonymity should always be able to go there.

114515.explanation: The municipality depends on the national government and/or the province in this matter. As long as there is no definitive policy, the municipality cannot allow holiday homes as a permanent residence. If the national government/the province cooperate, some holiday parks can be converted to permanent housing.

114518.explanation: Absolutely disagree. The poor become poorer and the rich richer. It cannot be that people with a low income lose this debt relief. Families with children can have a really hard time. As a municipality, we must support this group so that children grow up without poverty.

114521.explanation: It is important to stimulate and facilitate electric driving. However, the pace must be considered; there cannot be too few regular parking spaces while electric parking spots remain empty. We must closely monitor developments.

114522.explanation: For safety and clarity, it can help if the maximum speed is 30 km/h everywhere within the built-up area. An important point is that the roads must be adapted accordingly. This takes time and money, so it cannot be realized all at once.

114523.explanation: It is important that people receive care as much as possible from someone who fits their identity. Recognition and acknowledgment often increase trust and benefit the quality of care.

114524.explanation: Of course, it is important to pay attention to price. But quality and good (measurable) results of youth care are more important and often cost-saving in the long term.

114525.explanation: No resident likes this. But if we want to maintain livability in the towns in the coming years and keep the island clean, safe, and beautiful, we must all pay more for it. By increasing property tax, those with the greatest ability to pay bear the heaviest burdens.

114529.explanation: We must cherish the day of rest. No 24/7 economy on our island. We grant everyone a peaceful Sunday to catch their breath, go to church, visit family, and relax. That is why we do not want shopping Sundays on the island.

114530.explanation: As long as the problems with overcrowded centers are not solved nationally, all municipalities must participate. It concerns a limited percentage of the homes, and doing nothing solves nothing but only worsens the problems nationwide, including on the island. Proper distribution is important.

114531.explanation: It already is, at least the first six times. That is more than enough for a normal household. It is important to keep it free so that residents are encouraged to bring waste to the recycling center, ensuring good separation and maximal recycling. And no dumping.

114532.explanation: We must be careful with our good agricultural soil. Especially since not all roofs are yet covered with solar panels, this is not the first choice. It changes if a farmer combines crops with solar panels, creating a win-win situation.

114535.explanation: It is necessary for every child to obtain a swimming diploma on our island surrounded by water. However, school swimming has almost everywhere stopped for many reasons. It would also be a huge expense. We are glad that children from low-income families can still get a diploma thanks to the Child Package, and we will keep this!

114536.explanation: That is quite a sympathetic idea, and a nice lesson package or something similar could be created, for example by the regional museum. Although it is not easy because the dialect on the island is not the same everywhere. Beautiful to cherish, but it is up to the schools themselves whether they take this up.

114538.explanation: High flats do not fit with our island; the rural area is beautiful, and the view should not be spoiled by flats. Apartment buildings of around four stories are the maximum for our island. Depending on the location, it must be appropriate and the protected village view must be preserved.

114539.explanation: This hardly happens in our municipality and does not seem to pose a problem. Therefore, it is good to leave this choice to the landlord.

114542.explanation: If additional places come at the expense of play areas or greenery, we do not think that is a good idea.

114543.explanation: As far as we are concerned, the center of Ouddorp will become car-restricted. So that people with mobility issues still have options, but otherwise it becomes a nice green center where you can walk, do shopping, or enjoy a terrace. Attractive for residents and visitors.

114546.explanation: For the coming years, this is important. There have been years when primarily expensive houses were built; now it is time to correct that. Afterwards, the balance can be sought again, as it is always good to have a broad range so everyone can find a suitable place.

114547.explanation: Investing money in things like this is not allowed. However, the municipality can try to maintain facilities by supporting and facilitating. It would be great if every town had such a facility, large or small, possibly combined with care/day activities. It greatly increases livability.

114485.explanation: A referendum often simplifies complex issues into a yes/no choice and is costly. Not everything is suitable for this. We prefer strong citizen participation, such as citizen assemblies, themed participation, and good, early information to residents.

114489.explanation: The municipality supports residents with energy poverty, insulation challenges, and sustainability. The energy transition means we increasingly generate, store, and use local green energy. Energy communities must therefore be stimulated as much as possible. That is the future.

114491.explanation: The municipality of GO has the legal obligation to implement the national distribution law including emergency shelter. There is a quota attached so that asylum seekers are fairly distributed across the country.

114493.explanation: This cannot always occur on the farmer's own property. We also favor separating the employment contract and the rental contract. Larger locations for labor migrants can relieve the regular housing market.

114501.explanation: This is too simplistic. Tailored solutions are needed. The province does not allow farmland to be fully developed. We prefer (new) houses attached to village centers. There are occasional possibilities.

114503.explanation: The local broadcaster Archipel receives a (modest) subsidy in accordance with the national standard.

114505.explanation: All schools can participate in the Kunsttaart, which includes a music offering and the Music Carousel, where music teachers give lessons at school. Additionally, we support the GO music school with a subsidy and students can receive individual support through the Child Package if needed.

114509.explanation: BOAs have a role as safety brokers and local enforcers complementary to regular police tasks. More or less is not a goal in itself. Parking nuisance, dog waste, litter, and vandalism must be addressed.

114510.explanation: The legislator has attached strict rules to camera surveillance. We comply with these. However, we see value in occasional camera surveillance in vulnerable locations.

114511.explanation: We support various organizations currently building different types of shelters for women and children. Preferably local rather than somewhere in Spijkenisse.

114515.explanation: Tailored solutions are desired, with a strong role for the municipality. Not every holiday park is a residential neighborhood, but there are parks suitable for the regular housing market. (extra costs are for the owners)

114518.explanation: GroenLinksPvdA firmly supports retaining this arrangement.

114521.explanation: Yes, the growth of electric driving is increasing. Smart charging and shared charging points (private and public) are being promoted.

114522.explanation: In terms of livability, public health, and safety, this is a good goal and achievable in many villages. Good for pedestrians and cyclists.

114523.explanation: All care must be of good quality and neutral. That means everyone should feel at home with our care providers and an open attitude is expected from them. Specific care based on religion is possible in some cases but never with increased costs.

114524.explanation: Quality, effectiveness, and proximity are more important weighting factors.

114525.explanation: Goeree-Overflakkee is low compared to the national average. To maintain facilities as the national government cuts municipal budgets, an increase will also be necessary. But making choices and discontinuing or economizing facilities is equally important.

114529.explanation: Within the limits of tourist facilities, shops (and swimming pool) may be open on Sundays. GroenLinksPvdA respects the islanders' wish for Sunday rest. We believe employees must not be obligated to work on Sundays.

114530.explanation: This is still the statutory arrangement and we adhere to it. We focus on much more social housing, as we see the urgency and distressing situations in tenants' housing market on the island.

114531.explanation: The current arrangement offers up to 6 times per year free waste disposal at the recycling center. We want to keep it that way for now.

114532.explanation: GroenLinksPvdA does not consider a ban, nor do they want to encourage it. Wind and solar complement each other well as sustainable energy sources, but solar panels on roofs are our preference.

114535.explanation: No swimming lessons during school hours. Every child deserves a swimming diploma and we therefore support the new swimming pool. For families who cannot afford this themselves, we have the Child Package.

114536.explanation: It is not a municipal task to concern yourself with the content of the curriculum. Nor is it a desirable task.

114538.explanation: We want to safeguard the village character and see possibilities here and there for 3- or 4-story buildings. Higher than now is possible in some locations. This can lower prices, make it easier for housing corporations to build, and make smart use of available space.

114539.explanation: We have mixed feelings on this point. Renting out rooms can be fine, especially in large homes or vacant farmhouses. At all times, house squeezing and exploitation must be prevented.

114542.explanation: Cyclists and pedestrians have priority over car traffic in the village centers.

114543.explanation: During the tourist season, Ouddorp is already car-free.

114546.explanation: We must build according to needs and there is the greatest need for affordable housing. Housing is not a market, but a basic right.

114547.explanation: As sympathetic as it is, this is not a core task of the municipality. Moreover, due to legislation, the municipality may not support commercial activities with public money. However, thinking along with an entrepreneur is of course allowed!

114485.explanation: We encourage direct democracy, such as a binding corrective referendum. For a solid and fair decision, we exclude financial issues and use an outcome threshold.

114489.explanation: We do not solve grid congestion with waiting lists, but with smart systems. Energy hubs, neighborhood batteries, and smart charging in our villages make the grid more flexible and allow space for innovation. We therefore support initiatives of this kind.

114491.explanation: Everyone deserves a humane and stable start. That is why we want asylum seekers to learn the language quickly and be able to do volunteer work. This way they build their own future and contribute to our community.

114493.explanation: Labor migrants must be able to live with dignity. Those who live with their employer risk losing their housing if they lose their job, which leads to additional problems. To regulate the total number of places, D66 wants to make the rental of homes to labor migrants subject to a permit requirement.

114501.explanation: D66 voted against the policy rule for small-scale housing construction in the countryside. We especially want to expand villages at their edges to achieve more concentration of inhabitants and facilities. Building spread out in the countryside would actually dilute this.

114503.explanation: Journalists bring local politics to our residents. By doing so, they strengthen our democracy. D66 wants to strengthen local journalism without affecting the independent position of journalists, for example by establishing a fund with an independent board.

114505.explanation: Municipal subsidies make a rich school day possible in which every primary school child can have music lessons. We find it important that every child can develop themselves.

114509.explanation: We primarily want to focus on prevention and uniformed enforcement officers (boa's) are not alone: safety is also a task of the police. Should additional efforts be necessary to guarantee safety, we will take those measures.

114510.explanation: D66 wants to use cameras only where they are effective and proportionate against undermining or nuisance. A generic expansion would excessively burden residents’ privacy and create false security.

114511.explanation: D66 wants fast shelter, accessible reporting points, and psychological help for domestic violence. Local shelter strengthens safety.

114515.explanation: Many bungalow parks in Goeree-Overflakkee are suitable for permanent residence. The national and provincial governments are developing policies to legalize this. D66 also believes this should be possible within certain frameworks to relieve the regular housing market.

114518.explanation: This increases the risk that more residents will fall into debt and leads to longer municipal support through social assistance and other schemes.

114521.explanation: In the future, there will be more electric cars. It is important that Goeree-Overflakkee also takes this development into account and facilitates the greening of mobility in Goeree-Overflakkee.

114522.explanation: We improve traffic safety in village centers by introducing clear 30 km/h zones and well-lighted routes.

114523.explanation: D66 stands for inclusive and accessible care with freedom of choice and a human approach. Appropriate care increases trust and effectiveness.

114524.explanation: We find it important that every family has one point of contact (coordinator). Strong networks and appropriate support always take precedence over the lowest price. D66 wants quality and results to be central.

114525.explanation: In case of deficits, D66 wants to make efforts at the municipal level to always first look at how spending can be responsibly reduced before resorting to tax increases.

114529.explanation: Sunday is for many people a day of relaxation, meeting others, and free time. At that time, residents want the ability to do their shopping or visit a store. D66 believes shops should therefore be allowed to open on Sundays.

114530.explanation: Status holders receive an urgency declaration to enable moving on (from shelter), just like local home seekers get in urgent situations. Without this, integration is delayed, asylum shelter stalls, homelessness increases, and pressure on facilities grows.

114531.explanation: D66 applies the 'polluter pays' principle. We do encourage cooperation with thrift shops to prevent products from being thrown away unnecessarily. We do advocate targeted exemptions for people who cannot afford to pay.

114532.explanation: Placing solar panels on land is inefficient use of space. We prefer to see solar panels on building roofs.

114535.explanation: Swimming is important in our water-rich environment. However, we expect schools and/or parents to contribute to transportation costs.

114536.explanation: It would be great if children also learned something of the Flakkee dialect. The municipality must help schools pay attention to this so that the dialect is preserved as heritage.

114538.explanation: High-rise buildings affect the characteristic broadness of the landscape and detract from the identity of our historic cores. The height of buildings within existing village boundaries must therefore fit the current buildings.

114539.explanation: In a tight housing market, we need all forms of affordable housing. We do ensure clear rules and good livability so that room rentals can take place responsibly and safely.

114542.explanation: We continue to apply a generous parking standard in new construction projects (the minimum number of parking spaces that must be provided depending on the housing type). However, we encourage other forms of transport with the STOMP principle (walking/biking/public transport).

114543.explanation: We support the vision established after a poll among village residents, which includes the closure of the ring road from April to October. This brings more greenery and space for pedestrians to the center.

114546.explanation: D66 wants affordable housing and focuses on starters, families, and seniors. We do this by building extra social rental homes and offering other creative forms of housing.

114547.explanation: In recent years, many facilities have disappeared from our village centers. D66 wants 1% of the municipal budget to be controlled by the residents themselves. Each village can then decide how to make their neighborhood greener, more social, or healthier with that budget.

114485.explanation: The distance between politics and residents of Goeree Overflakkee is greater than desirable. We are in favor of a referendum when a subject has major societal impact.

114489.explanation: It is the residents’ own choice; we prefer to see community money spent on other initiatives.

114491.explanation: We comply with the legal requirements.

114493.explanation: Labor migrants must be housed on the employer’s own agricultural or horticultural site, or accommodated in existing housing for labor migrants.

114501.explanation: We have a housing shortage and each application must be considered to determine whether building is desirable here.

114503.explanation: The local broadcaster (regional broadcaster) already receives an additional subsidy from the Stimuleringsfonds voor de Journalistiek. From 2028, this will be fully taken over by the national government.

114505.explanation: Children with parents/caregivers on social assistance levels can apply through the Child Package.

114510.explanation: In places where safety is at risk, camera surveillance should be possible.

114518.explanation: We must ensure that municipal taxes remain affordable for everyone.

114523.explanation: Given the tight labor market, this seems impractical to implement in practice.

114524.explanation: Quality and quantity must be balanced.

114529.explanation: We support conducting a support measurement among residents on this matter.

114530.explanation: They must register for housing just like the local population.

114531.explanation: Residents may use the waste disposal site free of charge six times per year.

114532.explanation: We support solar panels on the roofs of homes and businesses, not on valuable land.

114535.explanation: We live in a water-rich environment and find it important that all children can swim.

114536.explanation: We are proud of our dialect and must preserve it.

114546.explanation: We believe building should be for all residents, not just the happy few. In this way, we hope to enable the (local) population to live on the island we are proud of.

114547.explanation: By building as much as possible in small villages, we ensure that livability is maintained and the existence of the supermarket, etc., is guaranteed.

114485.explanation: We are in favor of direct influence from citizens.

114489.explanation: Local initiatives should be approached positively.

114491.explanation: As a municipality, we are obliged to shelter asylum seekers. We stand for modest accommodation.

114493.explanation: The entrepreneur is primarily responsible for this. In return, regulations will be handled flexibly to make this possible.

114501.explanation: Evaluating local initiatives on a case-by-case basis is a tailored approach.

114503.explanation: What does the local broadcaster mean for our island and the residents? That should be leading in the discussion. At this time, the broadcaster receives subsidies.

114505.explanation: Music lessons are good for the child's development.

114509.explanation: The community enforcement officer should be more visible and accessible to citizens by holding consultation hours in every village.

114510.explanation: Where necessary, camera surveillance should be used effectively and based on information. If that means more (mobile) cameras, so be it. Street safety and tackling nuisance-causers come first.

114511.explanation: Providing safety is a basic need. If it appears that the current system does not function sufficiently, we must consider how to organize it better. If that means on the island, then that is preferred.

114521.explanation: As needed, if it becomes structurally clear that the number of parking spaces with charging points is insufficient.

114523.explanation: Providing care is the priority; other matters are secondary.

114531.explanation: Under current policy, you may already dispose of waste for free a number of times per year.

114547.explanation: Tailored solutions and livability are important. The municipality can facilitate making it attractive for an entrepreneur to invest.

statements.title: The municipality must invest money to preserve a supermarket, ATM and parcel office in every village.
The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: A women's shelter for victims of domestic violence should be established on Goeree-Overflakkee.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: Tall residential apartment blocks should be allowed in Middelharnis.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality must not allow Sunday shopping.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Residents of Goeree-Overflakkee must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Goeree-Overflakkee moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety should be deployed on Goeree-Overflakkee.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance on Goeree-Overflakkee.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should enable all primary schools to give attention to the local dialect.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The centre of Ouddorp should become car-free.
Goeree-Overflakkee should primarily have affordable rental and owner-occupied housing built.
